optionalfeature.name: Air Lift
entries: You can use an action to increase your vertical jump distance to 15 feet until the end of your turn. When you jump in this way, you can also glide with the updraft, allowing you to move 10 feet horizontally for every 5 feet you descend vertically.

optionalfeature.name: Detect Magic Savant
entries: Whenever you cast {@spell detect magic|sns}, you can choose one of the following benefits to amplify the spell.

items: Lengthened Detect Magic: The duration of {@spell detect magic|sns} is increased by 10 minutes.
Extended Detect Magic: The detection radius of {@spell detect magic|sns} is increased by 30 feet.
Penetrating Detect Magic: The spell can penetrate most barriers, and it is only blocked by 2 feet of stone, 2 inches of common metal, a 1-inch sheet of lead, or 6 feet of wood or dirt.

optionalfeature.name: Eidetic Memory
entries: Your memory is nearly perfect. You can accurately recall anything you've read or seen in the past month. Additionally, when retreading a travel route you've traveled in the past month, you have advantage on ability checks made against environmental hazards on that route, unless the landscape has been significantly altered in that time.

optionalfeature.name: Illusion Detective

prerequisite.other: At least one illusion spell in your spellbook.

entries: You have advantage on Investigation checks made against illusion, and attacks made against you by illusion have disadvantage. When you succeed on an ability check to see through an illusion, or an attack from one fails to score a standard or higher success, all allies within 30 feet of you gain advantage on checks to see through that illusion for the next minute.

optionalfeature.name: Loremaster of Creatures
entries: You are well-studied on naturally occurring creatures of the world. When you choose this study, choose one creature type: beasts, constructs, dragons, elementals, fey, fiends, giants, oozes, plants, or undead. This creature type becomes your Loremaster Creature Specialty. When you encounter a creature of the same type as your Loremaster Creature Specialty, you automatically know any history, legends, or myths related to it.

optionalfeature.name: Loremaster of Travel
entries: You are well-travelled and well-studied. You have advantage on History checks to recall information relating to myths and legends that are location-based. In addition, when you arrive at a settlement for the first time, choose one of the following subjects: culture, etiquette, government, or populace. The DM will give you a useful fact about that subject. You can take an expertise die on ability checks related to that subject in this settlement.

optionalfeature.name: Persistent Mending
entries: When you finish a long rest, choose up to 6 creatures who rested with you, including yourself. Choose one of the following pieces of their equipment to enchant with a persistent mending effect. The chosen item instantly fixes itself if it becomes damaged. Each item also grants an additional benefit. This effect lasts for 12 hours.

items: Coat: Choose either hot or cold weather. The creature ignores the penalties of that kind of weather on their journey activities.
Goggles: The creature's vision can never be reduced to less than 20 feet due to inclement weather.
Shoes: The creature gains an expertise die on Force March journey activity checks.

optionalfeature.name: Presto, Prestidigitation!
entries: If you are not actively casting {@spell prestidigitation|sns} or concentrating on a spell, the magic of {@spell prestidigitation|sns} is always keeping you, your clothes, and your gear sparkling clean.
The following effects are added to the list of options you can choose from when you cast {@spell prestidigitation|sns}.

items: You transmute a small piece of string in your hands into a sturdy 30-foot long rope. For the duration, it functions as a regular hempen rope.

Using the heads side of a coin like a lens, you can hold the coin over one of your closed eyes and see through it. While looking through the coin in this way, you gain darkvision to a range of 30 feet. The coin's darkvision effect ends when the spell ends.

A pebble you touch becomes a sensor that you can use to see through. For the duration as long as you are within 10 feet of the pebble, you can use an action to see through the pebble to a range of 30 feet. While seeing through the pebble, you are {@condition blinded|sns} and {@condition deafened|sns} with regard to your own senses.

On a piece of parchment or paper, you can create an exact copy of another piece of parchment or paper, including any writing, drawings, or other markings that appear on the original. The paper does not change size to accommodate any differences in size between the two. The copy you create lasts for the duration of this spell, and disappears when the spell ends.

optionalfeature.name: Amphibious Combatant
entries: You gain a swimming speed equal to your Speed, and may spend 1 exertion to reroll a failed Strength or Dexterity check related to aquatic activity (such as {@skill Athletics} checks to swim or {@skill Finesse} checks to keep balance on a ship's deck). You may choose whether to use the new roll or the original. In addition, you add your proficiency bonus to the number of minutes you can hold your breath before suffocating, and can spend 1 exertion to hold your breath for additional minutes equal to your proficiency bonus

optionalfeature.name: Burst of Strength
entries: You have moments of incredible strength. You may spend 1 exertion to add your proficiency bonus to a Strength check, even if you already add your proficiency bonus to the check.

optionalfeature.name: Campaigner
entries: You have the ability to go for the long haul, carrying heavy equipment across long distances. You add your Strength modifier an additional time to your Brawn checks. You may also reduce the duration of journeys by 1 day, to a minimum of 1. This must be decided at the start of the journey and the decision cannot be changed throughout it.

optionalfeature.name: Clearsight Sentinel
entries: You gain 60 feet of darkvision. In addition, you do not suffer disadvantage on Perception checks in dim light.

optionalfeature.name: Extreme Leap
entries: When you take the Dash action and make a long jump after moving no less than 20 feet, your jump distance is doubled for the turn. You may spend 1 exertion to triple your jump distance for the turn instead, and may move the full distance of your jump even if it exceeds your Speed.

optionalfeature.name: Mountaineer
entries: You gain a climbing speed equal to your Speed. In addition, moving through non-magical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement.

optionalfeature.name: Nightwatch
entries: You are used to sleeping light and making the most of your rest. When taking a long rest, add your proficiency bonus to the number of hours you can spend in light activity. In addition, one hour of that time can be spent in strenuous activity near the rest site (such as patrolling, fighting, or other adventuring activities) without interrupting your rest.

optionalfeature.name: Stable Footing
entries: A steady stance and careful footwork is instinctive to you. You have advantage on any check or save to avoid falling {@condition prone|sns} or handle difficult ground (such as against a {@spell grease|sns} spell or slippery ice), and any non-armour attacks against you attempting to do similar things to you have disadvantage. You are never {@condition prone|sns} after taking fall damage, and when calculating fall damage you treat the fall distance as if it were shorter by a number of feet equal to your proficiency bonus x 10.

optionalfeature.name: Weather Beaten
entries: Exposed to all kinds of weather over your life, you are inured to all but the most deadly effects. You gain advantage on saves against cold and hot weather, as well as any adverse effects from wind or precipitation, and such effects have disadvantage on attacks against you.

optionalfeature.name: Aerial Surveyor
entries: You are at home soaring at dizzying heights and peering towards the horizon. You can familiarises yourself with a 5-mile radius area around you by spending a minute studying your surroundings while flying or at great heights. Once your familiarise yourself with an area in this way, you gain an expertise die on any Perception or Survival checks you make while outdoors in that area for the next 24 hours, and you cannot familiarise yourself with another area until you finish a long rest. This does not grant any bonuses to journey activities.

optionalfeature.name: Aquatic Delver
entries: You are at home in the depths, able to control your breathing and attune to the subtle movements of the water. You gain a swimming speed equal to your Speed and you add your Wisdom modifier to the number of minutes you can hold your breath before suffocating. In addition, while fully submerged in water you gain blindsight out to a range of 10 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Cavern Skulker
entries: You've grown accustomed to the echoing solitude to vast caverns. You always know the actual direction of any sound you hear regardless of any echoes. In addition, while you are in complete darkness and not {@condition deafened|sns} you gain blindsight out to a range of 15 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Desert Dweller
entries: Few choose to willingly live where the sun relentlessly burns overhead but you find tranquility amongst the sands. You are naturally acclimatised to hot weather and extremely high temperatures, allowing you to ignore the penalties of hot weather on your journey activities. In addition, while in a desert or similar arid environment and a member of your party scores a substantial success or higher on a Harvest journey activity check, you gain 1 Supply. Once you use this benefit, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Survivor
entries: You've grown to know the lands twisted by the fey, distorted by magical pollution, or places otherwise warped in unnatural ways. You may make Survival checks in place of Magiscience checks regarding magical environments, foodstuffs, and creatures, and gain an expertise die on any checks made this way. This affects your Experiment journey activity checks.

optionalfeature.name: Herbal Apothecary
entries: Forests and jungles are filled with natural medicines ripe for the picking. You gain an expertise die on checks made to locate herbal ingredients. This affects your Harvest journey activity checks. In addition, whenever you gain something from your Harvest journey activity checks, you gain double the amount. This doubling does not stack with other sources of the same effect.

optionalfeature.name: Marshland Guide
entries: Bogs and swamps are unpleasant to most but are filled to with natural beauty to you. Moving through swamps, bogs, or muddy difficult terrain costs you no extra movement and you cannot become naturally lost while traveling through marshlands. In addition, you ignore the effects of roughing it imposed by resting in wet or swampy environments, allowing you to always consider these environments Havens.

optionalfeature.name: Master Forager
entries: No matter the brush you always know how to harvest nature's bounty. You have advantage on checks made to locate or harvest edible flora in the wild, which affects the Harvest journey activity, and whenever you would gain something from your Harvest journey activity checks, you gain twice the amount. This doubling does not stack with other sources of the same effect.

optionalfeature.name: Mountain Climber
entries: You gain a climbing speed equal to your Speed. You gain an expertise die on any Athletics checks made while climbing and you are also acclimated to high altitudes, including elevations above 20,000 feet. In addition, you ignore the effects of roughing it imposed by resting in rocky or uneven terrain, allowing you to always consider these environments Havens.

optionalfeature.name: Tundra Explorer
entries: You embrace the cold of snowbound tundra. You are naturally acclimatised to cold weather and extremely low temperatures, allowing you to ignore the penalties of cold weather on your journey activity checks, and you ignore the disadvantage to Perception checks imposed by blizzards. In addition, you ignore the effects of roughing it imposed by resting without warmth in environments of extreme cold, allowing you to always consider these environments Havens.

optionalfeature.name: A Better Mousetrap
entries: There's always a better trap design if you put your mind to it. You can improve traps as you set them. Traps you improve in this way always have their damage die size increased by 1. This affects trap weapons. Additionally, whenever you score a success or higher on the Hunt & Gather journey activity, you add a {@dice d6} to the amount of Supply you (and only you) found.

optionalfeature.name: Alchemical Prodigy
entries: You are a master of the alchemical arts and new mixes and concoctions come naturally to you. When you first gain this field discovery, you gain two schematics of your choice and add them to your schematic book. These schematics must be for potions of common or uncommon rarity (https://a5e.tools/magic-items?field_mi_category_target_id%5B%5D=302&field_mi_cost_value%5Bmin%5D=&field_mi_cost_value%5Bmax%5D=&combine=). In addition, you have advantage on checks made to create schematics for potions.

optionalfeature.name: Golden Ratio
entries: You've been taking exact measurements of both natural and magical specimens, and believe true expressions of both can be reduced down to mathematics. When you select this field discovery you may select two {@filter 1st-level spells from the druid spell list|spells|level=1|class=Druid}. These are considered artificer spells for you and you may add them to the list of spells you can prepare. If you are at least a 7th-level artificer when you select this field discovery, you may select 1st- or 2nd-level spells from the druid spell list instead. You may select this field discovery multiple times, choosing different druid spells each time.

optionalfeature.name: Modern Comforts
entries: With a little reengineering technology can alleviate all manner of discomforts. Whenever you begin a long rest you may repurpose one of your currently prepared spell inventions. If you do, that long rest counts as being in a Haven. You can determine the exact nature of this repurposed spell invention (some examples include using a {@spell burning hands|sns} spell invenction as a space heater or a {@spell shocking grasp|sns} spell invention as a bug zapper).

optionalfeature.name: Orienteering
entries: You always know to have a bit of magnet and a pin on you for orienteering emergencies. With 1 minute's work you can cobble together a makeshift compass. Compasses made in this way become useless after 1 hour and have no sale value. You're also adept at surveying your surroundings and you gain an expertise die on checks made for the Chronicle and Scout journey activities. In addition, you gain proficiency in Survival.

optionalfeature.name: Reengineering Nature
entries: An orderly mind can see all sorts of uses within the natural world. You may use Magiscience in place of Survival for the Cook journey activity.

optionalfeature.name: Retraced Development
entries: Even if dumped into the wilderness with nothing you'd get a laboratory back up and running in no time. With 1 hour of work you can create ramshackle versions of your tools of artifice. You take a -2 penalty on checks made with these ramshackle tools and they have no sale value.

optionalfeature.name: Rope and Pulley Master
entries: Numerous logistic puzzles can be solved with just a rope, a pulley, and proper application of intellect. By spending 10 minutes working with at least 300 feet of rope, you can create the following through ingenious use of ropes and pulleys.

items: A rope system that provides safe travel over a gap of 50 feet or less. You must be able to access both sides of this gap to create a rope system in this way.
A rope system that allows a creature to lift an object of a weight up to 5x its carrying capacity directly up as far as 50 feet.
A rope system that allows a creature to drag an object of a weight up to 5x its carrying capacity as far as 50 feet.

A manual rope elevator that allows a single Medium-sized or smaller creature to travel up to 50 feet straight up and down as if it were difficult terrain (a tree, cliff wall, ceiling, or similar structure is required at the top of this elevator for the ropes to attach).

entries: You can also deconstruct and recover the rope used in any of these rope creations by spending 10 minutes tearing it down.

optionalfeature.name: Strategic Dismantling
entries: While you may have no special talent for detecting traps, you and your tools can definitely deal with them once they've been pointed out. You gain an expertise die in Magiscience checks to disable mechanical traps. In addition, if you trigger a trap while attempting to disarm it, its attacks have disadvantage against you.

optionalfeature.name: Tinker Toys
entries: Your mastery of locomotion and gearing allows you to easily infuse elements of motion into simple devices. As an action you can create and place a Tiny moving gadget called a tinker toy (such as a walking doll, flying whirlgig, or toy boat) out of 1 gold worth of materials. This tinker toy has 0 armour, 1 hit point, and your choice of a Speed of 10 feet, fly speed of 10 feet, or swim speed of 10 feet. When you place this device it begins moving in a straight line until it is destroyed, it is blocked by an obstacle, or you use a bonus action to pick it up again. Tinker toys made in this way weigh 1 kilogram, and can carry up to 500 grams worth of other devices or materials. Once you create a tinker toy in this way you cannot do so again until you finish a short rest, and any existing tinker toys you've created fall apart whenever you create a new one.

optionalfeature.name: Draw Nourishment
entries: You can consume things that most people wouldn't consider sustenance, though it's hardly pleasant. You can spend 3 Sorcery Points before eating a pound of just about anything organic - bark, grass, cactus flesh, bone, whatever's at hand - and so long as you can stomach the taste, it counts as 1 Supply for you as your body transmutes it into something more edible. A poisonous substance consumed in this way is still poisonous.

optionalfeature.name: Hot Air
entries: The source of your magic is strangely buoyant. Any time you have Sorcery Points remaining, your weight is reduced by half, and you have 50% resistance to fall damage. Additionally, while not wearing medium or heavy armour, you float on water.

optionalfeature.name: Lingering Touch
entries: When you encounter a shed piece of a creature (such as a lock of hair, bloody bandage, a scale, or a tooth), you you can touch that object and instantly attune to it. You can only be attuned to one such object at a time. If you are carrying the shed piece and sttuned to it when you see the creature from which it originated, you immediately know that it belongs to that creature, at which point the attunement ends. At the DM's discretion, you may learn additional cryptic information from being attuned to such things.

optionalfeature.name: Retrace
entries: By spending 1 minute in meditation, you can reflect on the magical aura you leave behind as you travel. After doing so, you can perfectly retrace the steps you've taken in the past 4 hours. This causes your minimum success tier on the Scout journey activity to be failure.

optionalfeature.name: Mage Hunter
entries: When tracking a creature that can cast at least 1 spell, you can attempt to do so by focusing on the mystic energy it leaves in its wake, allowing you to use your Charisma for any ability checks made to track it. This does not benefit your journey activities.

optionalfeature.name: Magnetic Step
entries: You can move along vertical surfaces at half your Speed without needing to make an ability check. If you end your turn on such a surface, you immediately fall unless you have some other way to support yourself.

optionalfeature.name: Strange Traces
entries: While traveling, you can spend 1 sorcery point to obscure your trail. For the next hour, any tracks your group leaves become random, zigzagging, and incomprehensible. Creatures have disadvantage on Survival checks to track you and become lost on a failed check. This grants you advantage on the Cover Tracks journey activity.

optionalfeature.name: Ominous Insight
entries: When you know, you know, you know? When you fail a History, Magiscience, or Nature check, you can attempt to draw on your magics to find an answer and reroll the check. On a failure, your attempt clouds your mind and you have disadvantage on Intelligence checks until you finish a short rest. While suffering this disadvantage, you can't use Ominous Insight again.

optionalfeature.name: Wode Sense
entries: While traveling, any time you enter an illusory terrain or magic is used to cause you to become lost, you always notice within 1 minute of being affected.

optionalfeature.name: Beast Friend
entries: After a Befriend Animal journey activity, you can befriend and feed a Medium or smaller beast of your choice, becoming its guardian ranger (use the CR 1/8 generic minion - https://homebrewery.naturalcrit.com/share/4FjimI1VqkgS). It counts as a minion despite not requiring concentration. The beast won't attack except as a response to being attacked, but it can take other actions as normal and makes death saving throws. You can only be a guardian ranger to one beast at a time.

optionalfeature.name: Build Shelter
entries: You can change a failure on a Seek Shelter journey activity check into a success.
Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again for a week.

optionalfeature.name: Calls of the Wild
entries: Using verbal and nonverbal cues to communicate with beasts, you can gain basic information from them or make a simple request (like delivering a shrot message or small object).

optionalfeature.name: Ear to the Ground
entries: By expending 1 minute remaining stationary, using sound a vibrations alone, until you move or take an action you are able to deduce information for up to 30' in every direction, as if using blindsight or tremorsense.

optionalfeature.name: Expert Foraging
entries: After a Harvest journey activity, you can collect a number of edible, non-poisonous plants and grubs equal to your proficiency bonus. A creature can use its action to eat up to 5 plants or grubs, each of which restores 1 hit point. A creature who eats at least 1 also recovers 1 level of fatigue, but can only benefit from this once per long rest. The plants and grubs die and spoil 1 hour after being collected.

optionalfeature.name: Forced Marcher
entries: The journeys your party undertake are reduced in length by 1 day, to a minimum of 1 day.

optionalfeature.name: Guide
entries: While traveling, when you would have a failure for a Scout journey activity, you succeed instead, and you can Scout in addition to engaging in any other journey activity.

optionalfeature.name: Healing Salves
entries: On a success or higher on a Harvest journey activity, you can collect natural ingredients and concoct an ointment that speeds healing. This salve is potent for 24 hours and has a number of uses equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier. As an action, you can use the salve to restore {@dice 1d4} hit points to a creature within reach.

optionalfeature.name: Herbal Bitters
entries: On a success or higher on a Harvest journey activity, you can collect ingredients and brew two servings of a bitter drink that is potent for 24 hours. Imbibing a serving takes an action and reduces fatigue by one level for 4 hours.

optionalfeature.name: Huntsman
entries: You gain advantage on Gather Component journey activity checks.

optionalfeature.name: Longwalker
entries: When you would gain fatigue during a journey, you gain one fewer level of fatigue. Alternatively, you can choose to accept the level of fatigue, allowing up to two traveling companions to forgo gaining a level of fatigue instead. Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again for a week.

optionalfeature.name: Master Tracker
entries: You can spend 5 minutes looking at a 30' diameter outdoor area to study the ground and other clues. You learn all events which transpired in that area in the last 24 hours as though you had witnessed them yourself, although you do not learn secret information not obvious to an observer. The information you gain is only that which would be learned by sight; you do not gain sound-based information or learn what creatures in the area said to each other.
Once you have used this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Monster Mimic
entries: By imitating how a beast or studied adversary sounds, looks, and moves, you gain advantage on Deception and Intimidation checks made to convince a creature that it is seeing or hearing that beast or studied adversary.

optionalfeature.name: Poisons and Antidotes
entries: After a Gather Components journey activity, you can extract 3 doses of liquid poison (when ingested, a creature makes a save or is {@condition poisoned|sns} for 1 hour) from poisonous plants you've foraged. Alternatively, using the same plants you can spend an hour making a dose of antitoxin.

optionalfeature.name: Read the Room
entries: By spending 10 minutes observing facial expressions, body language, and vocal intonation, you can detect minute details which give you insight into the creatures within 30' of you. For the next minute, you can direct your focus on any one creature that you can see within 30'. If the creature you choose has an Intelligence of 3 or lower or doesn't speak any language, the creature is unaffected.
You learn the surface thoughts of the creature - what is most on its mind in that moment. As an action, you can shift your attention to another creature's thoughts.

optionalfeature.name: Relentless Pursuit
entries: Once you have successfully picked up the tracks of a creature, you are able to follow those tracks without any need for further checks for a period of up to 7 days.

optionalfeature.name: See the Unseen
entries: Once between long rests you can focus your attention on peripheral sights, sounds, and other signs of unseen passage. For the next hour, you can detect {@condition invisible|sns} creatures and objects, their location, movement, and actions, and even sense creatures and objects in the Ethereal Plane.

optionalfeature.name: Survivalist
entries: When traveling you may undertake two journey activities per day instead of one. Both activities must be from the following list: Befriend Creature, Gather Components, Harvest, Scout.

optionalfeature.name: Flurry of Blows
entries: While unarmed, you can spend 1 exertion as a bonus action to add double your weapon's base damage to the next melee weapon armour attack you make before the end of your turn.

optionalfeature.name: Long Step
entries: You can spend 1 exertion to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action on your turn, and your jump distance is doubled for that turn.

optionalfeature.name: Patient Defence
entries: You can spend 1 exertion to take the Block action as a bonus action on your turn.

optionalfeature.name: Empowered Attack (2)
entries: When you use the Attack action, your main hand weapon's base damage is tripled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 3d6} + triple your Strength modifier instead.

optionalfeature.name: Adept Weaponry
entries: Choose two weapons. If you are not proficient with them, you become proficient with them. They count as adept weapons for you.

optionalfeature.name: Battering Shield
entries: Your weapon attacks blend together so aggressively that your assault acts almost as a shield. When you use the Attack action and hit a creature, you gain a +2 bonus to armour against that creature until the beginning of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Battle Dance
entries: You gain proficiency in the Performance skill. As a bonus action, you can spend 2 exertion to bob and sway, starting a battle dance. Until the end of your turn, your Speed increases by 20 feet and opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage. When an opportunity attack fails to hit you, you can use your reaction to retaliate with an unarmed attack.

optionalfeature.name: Battle Meditation
entries: As an action, you can spend 1 Hit Die to regain {@dice 1d4} exertion. You can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Beyond Size
entries: You have learned to use your opponent's size against them. You can use basic maneuvers against creatures of any size.

optionalfeature.name: Closed Soul
entries: Whenever you are targeted by a non-armour attack, you can use your reaction and spend 2 exertion to grant disadvantage to all non-armour attacks from spells and other magical effects until the beginning of your next turn, including the triggering one.

optionalfeature.name: Dancing Maneuver
entries: When you use a bonus action to activate a combat maneuver, you can spend 2 exertion to start one of your dance focus features as part of the same bonus action.

optionalfeature.name: Deflect Missiles
entries: You can use your reaction to deflect or catch the missile when you are targeted by a ranged weapon armour attack. When you do so, the damage you take from the attack is reduced by {@dice 1d10} + your Dexterity modifier + your level.

If you end up taking 0 damage, you can catch the missile if it is small enough for you to hold in one hand. If you catch a missile in this way, you can spend 1 exertion to make a ranged weapon attack with the weapon or piece of ammunition you just caught, as part of the same reaction. The missile counts as an adept weapon for the attack, which has a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Deflect Spells
entries: When you are targeted by a ranged spell armour attack, you can use your reaction and spend 2 exertion to deflect the magic. When you do so, the damage you take from the attack is reduced by {@dice 2d10} + your Dexterity modifier + your level.
If you end up taking 0 damage, you can spend 1 exertion to redirect the spell to another target within 30 feet of you as part of the same reaction. The attack is governed by your Wisdom.

optionalfeature.name: Distant Death Dance
entries: As a bonus action, you can inhale air slowly and spend 2 exertion to start the distant death dance. Until the end of your turn, your unarmed reach increases to 60 feet. You have disadvantage on unarmed attacks against creatures more than 20 feet away from you.

optionalfeature.name: Dual Stance

prerequisite.other: know at least 2 stance combat maneuvers

entries: Your martial stances don't end when you start another stance. You can have two stances active at the same time. If you are knocked {@condition unconscious|sns} or begin a long rest both stances end.

optionalfeature.name: Eye for Detail
entries: You notice patterns others cannot. As an action, you can spend 1 exertion to study a creature you have watched speak for at least 1 minute. You can ask the DM one of these questions:

items: Is the creature hiding any strong emotions?
Does the creature have a secret agenda?
Did the creature lie in the last minute?
Is the creature hiding their true race and/or culture?

entries: The DM must give you a truthful answer.

optionalfeature.name: Focused Strikes
entries: Your unarmed strikes ignore bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing resistance and immunity.

optionalfeature.name: Forbidden Strike
entries: When you hit an opponent with an attack empowered by the Flurry of Blows focus feature, you can spend 1 exertion to deal additional damage equal to your martial arts die.

optionalfeature.name: Hooked Swords Dance
entries: When you are fighting with a shortsword and dual-wielding, you use a bonus action and spend 2 exertion to hook your weapons together to start a hooked swords dance. Until the end of your next turn, your shortsword gains the Reach trait and deals {@dice 1d10} additional slashing damage.

optionalfeature.name: Last Dance
entries: When the duration of one of your dance focus features ends, you can spend 2 exertion to make the effect last until the end of your next turn.
In addition, if you know two or more dance focus features you can spend 3 additional exertion to activate two dances using the same bonus action.

optionalfeature.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Spells and other magical effects have disadvantage with non-armour attacks against you.

optionalfeature.name: Maneuver Rush
entries: When you hit with a maneuver that has the Empowered Attack feature as a prerequisite, you can use another maneuver that does not make any attacks as part of the same action, at no exertion cost. You can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Mind Over Body
entries: You can use a bonus action and spend 2 exertion to gain temporary hit points equal to your martial arts die plus your level plus your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Mirage Dance
entries: As a bonus action, you can spend 2 exertion to swiftly start a mirage dance. Until the end of your next turn, you are under the effect of the {@spell mirror image|sns} spell.

optionalfeature.name: Staggering Strike
entries: You can deal staggering blows. When you score a major hit against a creature with a melee weapon armour attack, you can spend 2 exertion to {@condition staggered|sns|stagger} it until the end of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Powerful Blow
entries: As a bonus action, you prepare a crushing blow. If you make a melee weapon armour attack before the end of your next turn, you can spend 1 exertion to grant it additional damage equal to your martial arts die.

optionalfeature.name: Pressure Point Secrets

prerequisite.other: Debilitating Strike or Staggering Strike focus feature

entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon armour attack, you can spend 2 exertion to enable the use of either the Debilitating Strike or Staggering Strike focus feature on that attack.

optionalfeature.name: Purity of Body
entries: You can use your action to end one effect on yourself that is causing you to be {@condition poisoned|sns} or diseased.

optionalfeature.name: Share Focus
entries: As an action, you can share your spiritual fortitude with others. Choose a creature. That creature reduces the success tier of the next non-armour attack against it by one. You can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Shockwave
entries: You can use an action and spend 3 exertion to hit the ground so hard it sends a shockwave in a 40-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Make an area weapon reflex attack against each creature in that line that deals {@dice 3d10} bludgeoning damage and knocks targets {@condition prone|sns} on a hit.

optionalfeature.name: Stillness of Mind
entries: You can use your action to end one effect on yourself that is causing you to be {@condition charmed|sns} or {@condition frightened|sns}.

optionalfeature.name: Stunning Strike
entries: You can deal incapacitating blows. Whenever you score a major hit with a melee weapon armour attack, you can spend 1 exertion to {@condition deblitated|sns|debilitate} the target until the end of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Total Combat
entries: Whenever you successfully use a basic maneuver, the target suffers bludgeoning harm equal to your martial arts die.

optionalfeature.name: Undefeated
entries: When a creature would drop you to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to make an unarmed attack against any enemy within your reach. On a hit, you can spend 1 exertion to roll your martial arts die and gain that many temporary hit points. You can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Unlikely Wield
entries: You can wield two Offhand weapons in your offhand. This is a representation of you wielding the second abnormally, such as between your toes or in your mouth.

optionalfeature.name: Vengeful Spirit
entries: When you are about to make a death save, you can choose to set your own spirit loose as a vengeful ghost to stalk your prey, and automatically succeed on the save at the same time. The spirit acts on your initiative, after your turn, has your statistics, ethereal copies of your equipment, full hit points, and half your exertion pool. Your vengeful spirit is 50% resistant to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. It can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain, but it suffers {@dice 1d10} force harm if it ends its turn inside an object.

The vengeful spirit targets only the creature who reduced you to 0 hit points. When the creature is defeated, the spirit returns to your body. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Warding Dance
entries: When you are fighting unarmed, you can use a bonus action and spend 2 exertion to start a warding dance. Until the end of your next turn, you have 50% resistance against bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

optionalfeature.name: Weapon Skill
entries: Choose a martial weapon. You become proficient with that weapon and it counts as an adept weapon for you.

optionalfeature.name: Adept Speed
entries: Your Speed increases by 10 feet while you are unarmoured and not wielding a shield. You can choose this technique more than once. Its effects stack.

optionalfeature.name: Focus Speech
entries: A combination of your knowledge of body language and your spiritual awareness allows you to understand all spoken languages. Moreover, any creature that can understand a language can understand what you say.

optionalfeature.name: Gale Walk

prerequisite.other: Profiency with the Athletics skill; Adept Speed practiced technique

entries: You have advantage on Athletics checks made to jump. On your turn, you can spend 1 exertion to triple your jump distances until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Hurricane Walk
entries: Your step is so light you seem to float in the air. You can use a bonus action and spend 1 exertion to cast {@spell fly|sns} on yourself. You also have advantage on Stealth checks related to noise. The effect lasts until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Instant Step
entries: You can move so fast you seem to teleport. You can use an action to spend 4 exertion and choose an unoccupied space you can see within 500 feet. You teleport and arrive at exactly the spot desired. You can bring along your gear, carried items smaller than your arm, and a single creature of your size category or smaller. In addition, you become invisible until the beginning of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Marathon Runner
entries: You are used to running long distances. You add your martial arts die to Forced March journey activity checks.

optionalfeature.name: Nimble Athlete
entries: You can always choose to use your Dexterity modifier for Athletics checks.

optionalfeature.name: Powerful Finesse
entries: You can always choose to use your Strength modifier for Finesse checks.

optionalfeature.name: Religious Training
entries: You are proficient with the History skill. When you are in contact with a holy relic, religious artifact, or any kind of similar object or structure, you can spend 2 exertion to learn something about it. You receive a correct answer from the DM for a question about the object that can be answered with a yes or no.

optionalfeature.name: Shadow Walk
entries: You can step into a shadow and come out of another. When you are in dim light or darkness, as a bonus action you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in dim light or darkness. You have advantage on Stealth checks until the beginning of your next turn. This affects the Cover Tracks journey activity if you are the last in the party to undertake your journey activity for the day.

optionalfeature.name: Sixth Sense
entries: You have advantage on initiative rolls. In addition, you can always choose to use your Wisdom for Magiscience, History, and Nature checks. Finally, you gain an expertise die on your Investigation checks.

optionalfeature.name: Slow Fall
entries: You can use your reaction when you fall to reduce any falling damage you take by an amount equal to five times your level.

optionalfeature.name: Wall Walk

prerequisite.other: Proficiency with the Finesse skill; Adept Speed practiced technique

entries: You gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces on your turn without falling during the move. You can use a bonus action and spend 1 exertion to cast {@spell spider climb|sns} on yourself. This effect lasts until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Warrior's Awareness
entries: Your honed battlefield awareness is unmatched even in the wilderness. When you make a Survival check to avoid being lost, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your martial arts die.

optionalfeature.name: Water Walk
entries: You gain the ability to move across liquids on your turn without falling during the move. You can use a bonus action and spend 1 exertion to cast {@spell water walk|sns} on yourself. The effect lasts until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Wilderness Training
entries: You are proficient with the Survival skill. For the purposes of adept class features, you consider all beasts to be humanoids whose Strength or Dexterity is their highest ability score.

optionalfeature.name: Agonizing Blast
entries: When you use your Eldritch Blast, you deal extra damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.

optionalfeature.name: Armor of Shadows
entries: You are under the constant effects of the {@spell mage armour|sns} spell. This effect does not emit a magical aura.

optionalfeature.name: Beguiling Influence
entries: You gain proficiency in Deception and Persuasion. If you are already proficient in either of these skills, you instead gain an expertise die.

optionalfeature.name: Chains of Carceri
entries: You can cast {@spell hold monster|sns} on a celestial, fiend, or elemental without spending spell points or material components. You must finish a long rest before you can use this invocation on the same creature again.

optionalfeature.name: Cosmic Armory
entries: Your patron bestows you with a loot specialisation of your choice.

optionalfeature.name: Courts of the Outer Realms
entries: Choose one creature type from the following: aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, fiend, or undead. You are familiar with the intricacies of society and politics among those creatures, and you have advantage on checks made to interact with them or recall lore about them.
Additionally, you gain proficiency with your choice of either {@skill Magiscience} or {@skill History}. If you are already proficient in the chosen skill, you instead gain an expertise die.

optionalfeature.name: Deep Patronage
entries: Choose an additional Expanded Spell List. You learn all the spells on that list and they do not count against your spells known.

optionalfeature.name: Dreadful Word
entries: You learn the {@spell confusion|sns} spell, and the spell doesn't count against your number of spells known.

optionalfeature.name: Eigth Arcanum
entries: You learn an {@filter 8th-level warlock spell|spells|level=8|class=Warlock} that you can cast once between long rests.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Bind
entries: When you score a major success against a creature with your Eldritch Whip, it is {@condition staggered|sns} for 1 minute, or until it passes a passive save.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest. You also regain the use of this feature after you've spent 5 spell points to cast a spell or use other warlock features

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Elementalism
entries: When you use your Eldritch Blast, you can spend 1 spell point to choose acid, cold, fire, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, radiant, or thunder damage. Until you complete a short or long rest, your Eldritch Blast deals your choice of force damage or the chosen damage type.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Grasp
entries: When you score a major success against a Large-sized or smaller creature with your Eldritch Blast, you may grapple it. The grapple automatically ends if the creature leaves your Eldritch Blast range or if you attempt to grapple another creature.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Prism
entries: You can use an action to unleash your Eldritch Blast against creatures within a 20-foot cube originating at a point within 60 feet. Make a spell will attack against each creature in the area that deals force damage equal to a number of {@dice d12}s equal to your proficiency bonus.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest. You also regain the use of this feature after you've spent 5 spell points to cast a spell or use other warlock features.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Riposte

prerequisite.other: Eldritch Disturbance or Eldritch Ray feature

entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet hits you, you can use your reaction and spend spell points to use your Eldritch Disturbance or Eldritch Ray against it. Instead of the normal damage, you deal {@dice 1d10} force damage for every 1 spell point spent activating this feature (up to a maximum equal to your proficiency bonus).

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Severance
entries: You can use an action to unleash your Eldritch Blast against creatures within a 60-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Make a spell reflex attack against each creature in the area that deals force damage equal to a number of {@dice d12}s equal to your proficiency bonus.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest. You also regain the use of this feature after you've spent 5 spell points to cast a spell or use other warlock features

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Spear
entries: The range of your Eldritch Ray increases to 300 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Squall
entries: You can use an action to unleash your Eldritch Blast against all creatures within a 30-foot cone. Make a spell reflex attack against each creature in the area that deals force damage equal to a number of {@dice 1d12}s equal to your proficiency bonus.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest. You also regain the use of this feature after you've spent 5 spell points to cast a spell or use other warlock features

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Tentacle
entries: When you hit a creature with your Eldritch Blast, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight toward you.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Warrior
entries: You gain proficiency with medium armour and martial weapons.

optionalfeature.name: Entity of Myth
entries: You gain 50% resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from weapons not made from cold iron.

optionalfeature.name: Eyes of the Runekeeper
entries: You can read all writing.

optionalfeature.name: Fiendish Vigor
entries: You learn the {@spell false life|sns} spell, and the spell doesn't count against your number of spells known. When you cast it on yourself as a 1st-level spell, you can do so without spending spell points or material components.

optionalfeature.name: Frog Fangs
entries: Your familiar can take the Attack action. Attacks made by your familiar count as your attacks and deal additional force damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Gaze of Two Minds
entries: You can use your action to touch a willing humanoid and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn. On subsequent turns you can use a bonus action to maintain this connection, extending the duration until the end of your next turn. While perceiving through the other creature's senses, you benefit from any special senses possessed by that creature, and you are {@condition blinded|sns} and {@condition deafened|sns} to your own surroundings.

optionalfeature.name: Lifedrinker
entries: When you hit a creature with your pact weapon, the creature takes extra necrotic damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier (minimum 1).

optionalfeature.name: Mask of Many Faces
entries: You can cast {@spell disguise self|sns} without spending spell points.

optionalfeature.name: Minions of the Multiverse
entries: When you choose this invocation, choose one spell with the minion tag of your choice from any spell list, up to a spell level that you are capable of casting (maximum of 5th-level). You can cast this spell once by spending an appropriate number of spell points (see Table: Spell Points). You cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Mire the Mind
entries: You can cast {@spell slow|sns} once by spending 5 spell points. You cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Misty Visions
entries: You can cast {@spell silent image|sns} without spending spell points or material components.

optionalfeature.name: Noxious Invigoration
entries: Whenever you gain temporary hit points, increase the amount you gain by your level.

optionalfeature.name: Ominous Tormentor
entries: You gain proficiency in Insight and Intimidation. If you are already proficient in either of these skills, you instead gain an expertise die.

optionalfeature.name: Overclocked Concentration
entries: You can maintain concentration on two spells simultaneously. Their cumulative spell-level can be no higher than 5th-level. When you lose concentration both spells end.

optionalfeature.name: Pactkeeper's Reward
entries: You gain an additional Pact Boon of your choice.

optionalfeature.name: Penultimate Arcanum
entries: You learn a 9th-level warlock spell that you can cast once between long rests.

optionalfeature.name: Potent Witchcraft
entries: Your warlock cantrips deal extra damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.

optionalfeature.name: Reanimator
entries: You can cast {@spell animate dead|sns} once by spending 5 spell points; you can instead cast it as a 4th-level spell by spending 6 spell points, or as a 5th-level spell by spending 7 spell points. You cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Repelling Blast
entries: When you hit a creature with your Eldritch Blast, you can push the creature up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line.

optionalfeature.name: Sculptor of Flesh
entries: You can cast {@spell polymorph|sns} once by spending 6 spell points. You cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Seething Aura
entries: Choose one of the following damage types: cold, fire, lightning, necrotic, psychic, radiant. Whenever you spend spell points, you can choose to unleash energy that deals an equal amount of damage of the chosen type to hostile creatures within 15 feet of you.

optionalfeature.name: Seventh Arcanum
entries: You learn a 7th-level warlock spell that you can cast once between long rests.

optionalfeature.name: Sixth Arcanum
entries: You learn a 6th-level warlock spell that you can cast once between long rests.

optionalfeature.name: Sign of Ill Omen
entries: You learn the {@spell bestow curse|sns} spell, and the spell doesn't count against your number of spells known.

optionalfeature.name: Spellbinding Contract
entries: You can cast {@spell geas|sns} once by spending 7 spell points. You cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.
If the command of the geas is the stipulation of an agreement to which the target consents, the target automatically fails its save against this spell.

optionalfeature.name: Thief of Five Fates
entries: You learn the {@spell bane|sns} spell, and the spell doesn't count against your number of spells known.

optionalfeature.name: Tines of Wyrd
entries: Whenever a non-armour attack hits you, you can spend 5 spell points to reroll it and take the second result.

optionalfeature.name: Thirsting Blade
entries: When you use the Attack action, your pact weapon's base damage is doubled. For example, a weapon with a damage of {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier deals {@dice 2d6} + double your Strength modifier instead.

optionalfeature.name: Versatile Blast
entries: You learn an additional Eldritch Blast feature of your choice.
You can take this invocation a second and third time, each time learning a new Eldritch Blast feature.

optionalfeature.name: Voice of the Chain Master
entries: So long as you are on the same plane of existence, you can communicate telepathically with your familiar, perceive through your familiar's senses, and also speak through your familiar in your own voice (even if your familiar is normally incapable of speech).

optionalfeature.name: Whispers of the Grave
entries: You can cast {@spell speak with dead|sns} without spending spell points.

optionalfeature.name: Witch Sight
entries: You can see the true form of any shapechanger or creature concealed by illusion or transmutation magic while the creature is within 30 feet of you.

optionalfeature.name: Whirling Void
entries: You learn the {@spell black tentacles|sns} spell, and the spell doesn't count against your number of spells known.

optionalfeature.name: Ascendant Step
entries: You can cast {@spell levitate|sns} on yourself without spending spell points or material components.

optionalfeature.name: Beast Speech
entries: You are under the constant effects of the {@spell speak with animals|sns} spell. This effect does not emit a magical aura.

optionalfeature.name: Book of Ancient Secrets
entries: The complex sigils and symbols of ritual magic appear in your Book of Shadows. Choose three 1st-level spells that have the ritual tag. The spells appear in the book and don't count against the number of spells you know. You can't cast the spells except as rituals, unless you've learned them by some other means.

optionalfeature.name: Devil's Sight
entries: You can see normally in all types of darkness (including magical) to a distance of 120 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Sight
entries: You can cast {@spell detect magic|sns} without spending spell points.

optionalfeature.name: Inscrutability
entries: You are under the constant effects of the {@spell nondetection|sns} spell.

optionalfeature.name: Master of Myriad Forms
entries: You can cast {@spell alter self|sns} without spending spell points.

optionalfeature.name: Mirror, Mirror
entries: Using a reflective object, such as an ordinary mirror or the smooth surface of water, you can commune with your patron or their emissary. You may do so during a short or long rest, receiving or conveying information related to your master's bidding. This entity may also use such surfaces to bridge communication with you on its own terms.

You can also use this feature to cast {@spell augury|sns} without spending spell points. Casting the spell in this way does not require material components, although you do need a reflective surface by which your patron might appear to you. Once you cast the spell in this way, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: One with Shadows
entries: You can use an action to become {@condition invisible|sns} while you are in an area of dim light or darkness. Your invisibility lasts until you move, take an action, or use your reaction.

optionalfeature.name: Otherworldly Leap
entries: You are under the constant effects of the {@spell jump|sns} spell. This effect does not emit a magical aura.

optionalfeature.name: Portents and Portals
entries: You automatically know when you are within 1 mile of a portal or gateway to another plane, even if it is inactive or malfunctioning (but not destroyed).
You have advantage on checks made to locate this portal, and gain an expertise die on any checks you make to stabilize, activate, or open it.

optionalfeature.name: Shadowveil
entries: You can cast {@spell invisibility|sns} without spending spell points.

optionalfeature.name: Visions of Distant Realms
entries: You can cast {@spell arcane eye|sns} once without spending spell points. You cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.
At 15th level, you can instead cast {@spell arcane eye|sns} at will.

optionalfeature.name: Whiff of the Beyond
entries: You automatically know when an aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, fiend, or undead creature has been in a space within 30 feet of you within the past 24 hours, though not which type of creature triggered this knowledge.
Additionally, you gain an expertise die on Perception checks, and you have advantage on checks made to track these creatures.

optionalfeature.name: Bestowed Understanding
entries: When attempting to understand a language with which you are not proficient (whether spoken or written), you can make a DC 15 Insight check to understand the basic message. If the language is considered forgotten, you roll with disadvantage. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, and regain spent uses upon finishing a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Divine Health
entries: You are immune to disease and have advantage on saves made to resist the {@condition poisoned} condition.

optionalfeature.name: Divine Vision
entries: You gain 60 feet of darkvision. In addition, you always have the {@spell See Invisibility|sns} spell prepared and can use this feature once per long rest to cast it withuot expending a spell slot.

optionalfeature.name: Do Without
entries: Your divine connection and years of training combine to allow you to survive without Supply for a number of days in a row equal to your Charisma modifier without suffering any adverse effects.

optionalfeature.name: Exemplary
entries: You gain an expertise die on Athletics or Finesse checks made to climb, jump, run, and swim. This affects the Force March journey activity. When your party makes a group Athletics or Finesse check, you may apply the results of your roll to yourself and one ally. You can choose which ally to apply your result to after everyone has rolled, but must do so before the DM says whether you succeed or fail. This part of the lesson does not affect journey activities.

optionalfeature.name: Glorious Purpose
entries: When you agree to undertake a quest that specifically fulfills the tenets of your herald subclass, you gain proficiency with a skill that will be crucial to completing that quest. The skill is chosen by the DM. If you are already proficient with the skill, you gain an expertise die instead. The benefits of this divine lesson do not change until the quest has been completed.

optionalfeature.name: Heraldic Order
entries: Whenever you take a long rest in a major city, roll {@dice 1d6}. On a result of 4 or higher the merchants who you purchase your default supplies from recognise you as a herald of an order they either fear or respect and charge you nothing, resulting in you retaining 1gp. If you insist on paying, and therefore foregoing this gold, they may offer you information about individuals or places that might be of interest to your heraldic order.

optionalfeature.name: Miraculous Discovery
entries: You gain an expertise die on Investigation checks. Additionally, you can always choose to use your Charisma when making Investigation checks.

optionalfeature.name: Naturalist
entries: Choose one type of terrain: arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, subterranean, or swamp. You gain an expertise die on Nature and Survival checks made in this terrain. In addition, once each week when your party has run out of Supply, your next Hunt & Gather journey activity check scores at least a success.

optionalfeature.name: Prophetic Protection
entries: Whenever you would trigger a trap, the DM makes a secret Charisma (Perception) check for you against the DC to spot the trap. On a success, you do not spot the trap but get a strong sense that moving any further will put you in danger.

optionalfeature.name: Seek Truths
entries: You gain an expertise die on Insight checks made to discern the truth. This affects the Gossip journey activity. In addition, after you have made a successful Insight check opposed by a creature's Deception check, you have advantage on Insight checks made against it for 24 hours.

optionalfeature.name: Sense Import
entries: Your Divine Sense extends to detect whenever you enter a place that is or once was of great holy or unholy significance, and you learn basic information about why or to whom it was important. Additionally, you have advantage on checks made to learn more information about the area while you are there.

optionalfeature.name: Silvered Tongue
entries: You gain an expertise die on Intimidation and Persuasion checks. In addition, when you successfully intimidate or persuade a creature, you have advantage on Intimidation and Persuasion checks made against it for the next 24 hours.

optionalfeature.name: Undaunted
entries: While exploring a place which you have never been to before, you can use your reaction to gain advantage on ability checks and armour attack rolls until the end of your next turn. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, and regain spent uses upon finishing a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Ancestral Guidance
entries: In addition to the spells you normally prepare, you always have {@spell speak with dead|sns} prepared, and you can use this feature to cast it once between long rests without spending a spell slot. When you do so, you do not have to target a corpse; you can also target a grave marker, memorial, statue, painting, or other depiction of the deceased creature you wish to talk to. If the chosen creature is not dead, or if such a creature never existed in the first place, the spell fails.

optionalfeature.name: Compassionate Nurse
entries: Your insight and sympathy more than make up for your lack of formal medical training. You gain an expertise die on Medicine checks. In addition, you may always choose to use Wisdom when making Wisdom checks.

optionalfeature.name: Gentle Healer
entries: During a short rest, you can aid your allies in their recovery. A creature you choose may roll expended Hit Dice twice, taking the higher result. You may not expend your own Hit Dice while using this feature, since you use all of your energies to heal your companions.

optionalfeature.name: Graceful Fall
entries: Falling from a height greater than 30 feet causes your greater entity to intervene. You are protected as if by the {@spell feather fall|sns} spell. This sign does not use your reaction.

optionalfeature.name: Monastic Austerity
entries: Your greater entity acts as a buffer to shield you from hardship or your religious self-discipline sustains you even during harsh times, preventing the first level of {@condition fatigue|sns} you would take each day.

optionalfeature.name: Numinous Awareness
entries: When you see a creature that appears to be a beast, you automatically recognise whether it is a celestial, fiend, or fey. You have advantage on Magiscience and Medicine checks made to interact with magical wounds and maladies caused by celestials, fiends, and fey.

optionalfeature.name: Premonition
entries: You have the ability to interpret messages sent to you in dreams or visions by your greater entity. After each long rest you know either:

items: a piece of information that will help you in your endeavours before the end of your next long rest,

or, how one action will play out before the end of your next long rest. Roll a {@dice d20}. Before the end of your next long rest, you may choose to replace any attack roll, ability check, or save made by a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you with the result of this {@dice d20} roll.

entries: The DM lets you know which benefit is available whenever you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Preservation
entries: Your connection to a greater entity allows you to sense poison or disease in food, drink, or other consumables. You must have had direct contact with them or their container in order to sense this corruption.

optionalfeature.name: Righteous Path
entries: Divine intuition guides you through the tangled paths of constructed or inhabited areas without confusion. Once per long rest you may choose to walk the Righteous Path for 1 hour. While walking the Righteous Path, you are aware when enemies are within 60 feet of you, and you know if backtracking or alternate routes could avoid them. You can also use an action to sense if there are traps within 30 feet, but not the location or nature of the traps.

optionalfeature.name: Soothing Words
entries: Your divinely-inspired counsel and wise advice can calm troubled minds. You may reduce one ally's {@condition strife|sns} by one level. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Supernal Intuition
entries: Choose a creature type from fiend, fey, celestial, elemental, or undead. You know when a creature of this type is within 60 feet of you, although you do not know its location. The {@spell nondetection|sns} spell protects against this feature. You may select this sign multiple times, choosing a different creature type each time.

optionalfeature.name: Theologian
entries: Years of devoted learning have given you a deep understanding regarding the evolution of religion, theology, and the figures involved. You gain an expertise die on History checks. In addition, you may always choose to use Wisdom when making History checks.

optionalfeature.name: Voice of Doom
entries: Your personal knowledge of divine wrath and the end times make your proclamations utterly chilling. You gain an expertise die on Intimidation checks. In addition, you may always choose to use Wisdom when making Intimidation checks.

optionalfeature.name: Bewitching Companion
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks made using the chosen skill.
In addition, you gain an expertise die on checks made to influence anyone accompanying you on a journey.

optionalfeature.name: Brutish Confrontation
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks made using the chosen skill.
In addition, you gain advantage on ability checks made to prepare an ambush. This benefits the Hunt & Gather journey activity.

optionalfeature.name: Improvised Magiscience
entries: You gain proficiency with Magiscience. If you are already proficient with Magiscience, you instead gain an expertise die. In addition, you gain advantage on Magiscience checks made to build a temporary construction (such as a bridge or ladder) and shore up existing structures (like the wall of a collapsing building). This does not benefit the Experiment journey activity.

optionalfeature.name: Marching Song
entries: You know how to inspire and motivate your companions as they travel. When you start a journey, you may choose for the length of that journey to be reduced by 1 day, to minimum of 1, but prevent the use of the Cover Tracks journey activity by any creature on that journey.

optionalfeature.name: Profitable
entries: You gain proficiency with Persuasion. If you are already proficient with Persuasion, you instead gain an expertise die. Whenever you make a Persuasion check to earn coins through performance, you gain twice as much as normal. This doubles the perk points you gain from the Busk journey activity.

optionalfeature.name: Resonance
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks made using the chosen skill.
In addition, when either you or a companion casts a ritual spell, its duration is doubled and it affects twice the usual number of targets.

optionalfeature.name: Sly Confidant
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks made using the chosen skill.

In addition, when you succeed on an opposed Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma check, you gain advantage on your next opposed Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma check against the same creature. This benefits no journey activities.

optionalfeature.name: Song of Rest
entries: Any creatures able to perceive you performing your art while taking a short rest regain an additional {@dice 2d4} hit points.

optionalfeature.name: Showoff
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks made using the chosen skill.

In addition, when you succeed on an Athletics or Finesse check to climb, balance, leap over danger, or otherwise physically overcome an obstacle, a number of allies equal to your proficiency bonus gain advantage on the same check made to overcome the same obstacle.

optionalfeature.name: Townie
entries: Choose one skill you are proficient with. You gain an expertise die on checks made using the chosen skill. As long as you have a positive reputation in a town or city, all goods you would spend gold on cost 1/3rd less.

optionalfeature.name: Widely Known
entries: Your minimum level of prestige in any settlement is always at least somewhat known.

optionalfeature.name: Exacting
entries: You've won more battles armed with a sextant and your keen intellect than you have any blade. You gain an expertise die on Survival checks made to navigate, which does not benefit the Cook journey activity. At the start of a journey, you can make a DC 10 Survival check to navigate a faster route, reducing the length of the journey by 1 day (minimum 1) on a success.

optionalfeature.name: Keep Walking
entries: You can motivate your allies to keep going even when they are tired and hungry. You gain advantage on Force March journey activities.

optionalfeature.name: Lay of the Land
entries: Knowledge of the terrain can be the difference between victory and defeat. By spending 10 minutes observing the area in a 2-mile radius you can spend 2 exertion points to pick out where there are choke points, large swathes of cover, watercourses, vegetations that can offer concealment, ridgelines, and so on. You gain an expertise die on Magiscience and Survival checks made within the area, and on checks made to prepare an ambush or realise you are being ambushed. This benefits the Experiment, Cook, Cover Tracks, and Hunt & Gather journey activities on the same day that you spent the exertion.

optionalfeature.name: Rewarding Repute
entries: Your deeds have heroically won over the hearts and minds of people everywhere or cowed them into submission. Whenever you visit a settlement, the commoners there tell you all the valuable information they can about their including nearby ruins, the general environment of nearby wilderness, and how populated the region is. You gain an expertise die on Nature and Survival checks made within 1 day of any settlement you have visited. This affects your Befriend Animal and Cook journey activities.

optionalfeature.name: Team Tactics
entries: With your directions to help there is very little your companions can't accomplish. When more than one creature takes the Help action to aid an ally making an ability check, for each additional creature helping the check is made with a +1 bonus. Only a number of additional creatures equal to half your proficiency bonus are able to Help in this way.

optionalfeature.name: Teamwork
entries: When you are involved in a group check, all members of the check gain an expertise die. This can also be used for group Persuasion and Deception checks, where the final check gains an expertise die. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Agile Sprinter
entries: You are adept at traversing obstacles while running, able to efficiently and rapidly climb and leap between trees, vines, rooftops, and ropes. You gain an expertise die on Athletics and Finesse checks made to climb, run, and swing. This affects the Force March journey activity.

optionalfeature.name: Flock Step
entries: You are adept at reading the flow of creatures grouped together and can effortlessly sync into a herd or crowd. You gain an expertise die on Stealth checks made to hide amongst or blend in with a group of 5 or more creatures. This does not affect your journey activities.

optionalfeature.name: Lead the Pack
entries: Your unfaltering actions inspire your teammates to greater efforts. You gain an expertise die on Athletics or Finesse checks made to climb, jump, run, and swim. This affects the Force March journey activity. When you party makes a group Athletics check, you may apply the results of your roll to yourself and one ally. You can choose which ally to apply your result to after everyone has rolled, but must do so before the DM says whether you succeed or fail.

optionalfeature.name: Mark of the Wilderness
entries: You have traveled far and seen much, and those around you can tell. You gain an expertise die on Intimidation checks. In addition, you may always choose to use Strength when rolling an Intimidation or Persuasion check.

optionalfeature.name: Natural Tracker
entries: After making a connection with another creature, however tenuous, you make a connection that gives you an edge when pursuing it. After you spend at least 1 minute observing a creature, you gain an expertise die on checks made to track that specific creature.

optionalfeature.name: Path of Blustery Autumns
entries: Instead of fighting the sharp winds you have learned from them. You reduce the length of journeys your party undertakes by 1 day, to a minimum of 1. Additionally, you can choose to ignore difficult terrain outside of combat.

optionalfeature.name: Path of Drowning Springs
entries: The rains have taught you the ways of the overflowing rivers. You gain a swim speed equal to your Speed and can hold your breath for a number of minutes equal to 1 + twice your fortitude.
In addition, while swimming you can use a bonus action and spend one use of Rage to move your swim speed in a straight line.

optionalfeature.name: Path of Lean Winters
entries: The lessons of the snows have put you beyond mere cold and hunger. You ignore the penalties of cold weather on your journey activities. Additionally, you can go without Supply for a number of days equal to 3 + your fortitude (minimum 1) before suffering {@condition fatigue|sns}.

optionalfeature.name: Path of Scorching Summers
entries: By emulating the beasts of the desert you are above the concerns of heat and thirst. You ignore the penalties of hot weather on your journey activities. Additionally, whenever you would gain a level of {@condition fatigue|sns} from not consuming Supply, you may make a save, avoiding the {@condition fatigue|sns} on a success.

optionalfeature.name: Sharpened Senses
entries: Your time spent drawing on your rage has brought the world into sharp, relentless focus. You gain an expertise die on Investigation, Perception, or Survival checks made to follow a creature's trail.

optionalfeature.name: Apothecary Basics
entries: Whenever you take a short or long rest, you can use your medical knowledge to treat one creature. That creature can heal as if it had one extra {@dice 1d6} Hit Die during this rest, or it can ignore one level of {@condition fatigue|sns} for the next four hours.

optionalfeature.name: Deductive Tracker
entries: After observing a creature, you make a deduction that gives you an edge when pursuing it. After you spend at least 1 minute observing a creature, you gain an expertise die on checks made to track that specific creature.

optionalfeature.name: Hostilia Naturae
entries: Following the path trod by Drakran naturalist Karl Evol, author of A Theory of Noetic Field Adaptation Toward Terrifyingness Among Malice Ecologies, you have studied the many perils of wild flora and fauna. You gain an expertise die on checks to avoid, locate, or understand the abilities of aberrations, beasts, and monstrosities.

optionalfeature.name: Impromptu Persona
entries: You do not need preparation to come up with a convincing fake persona, background, and excuse to cover anything suspicious you are doing. You can gain advantage on Deception checks to pretend to be someone else. However, the ruse won't hold up to prolonged scrutiny. If you spend more than a few minutes in someone's presence, you must make a new Deception check with disadvantage to maintain the ruse.

optionalfeature.name: Just What Have We Gotten Ourselves Into?
entries: Whenever you start a combat surprised, you gain an expertise die on your initiative check. When you trigger a trap, you gain an expertise die to your defences for the first attack the trap makes against you, or on the first save you need to make. After you use this scheme, you cannot use it again until you complete a short rest.

optionalfeature.name: Kleptology
entries: When a creature makes a melee armour attack against you or otherwise touches you, you can make a Finesse check against them as a reaction.
Additionally, when you spend at least a minute interacting with someone while within arm's reach, you gain an expertise die on Finesse checks that involve them until the interaction ends.

optionalfeature.name: Local Informants
entries: You quickly can locate people willing to share information. If you spend an hour reaching out to local residents or simply perusing a recent periodical or newspaper, for the next day you gain an expertise die on History and Investigation checks related to the area.

optionalfeature.name: Meeting of Minds
entries: You gain an expertise die on Charisma checks when trying to interact with someone who has proficiency in either History, Magiscience, or Nature, so long as you also have proficiency in that skill.

optionalfeature.name: Motto of the Tropezaros
entries: You make sure you're always prepared. Once per short rest you can produce a non-magical item that you could have purchased or prepared at some point, small enough to fit in whatever storage you have available. This could range from a forged passport in your pocket to a glider stowed in the hold of a ship you're traveling on. Pay the appropriate price for it. Limits of causality apply.

optionalfeature.name: Physical Education
entries: Once per hour, when you succeed on an Athletics check to climb, swim, or jump you can offer advice and encouragement to your allies. For the next minute, each ally who attempts a check to traverse the same area gains an expertise die on the check.

optionalfeature.name: Read You Like A Spellbook
entries: You can spend an action to make a Magiscience check against the standard DC of a creature you can sense. If you succeed, you deduce from the creature's demeanor, fashion, and accoutrements what cantrips they know and the last non-cantrip spell they cast, if any.

optionalfeature.name: Reserved Seating
entries: You have forethought of when and where you might need to be places. If you knew you would be in a given area, once per day you can call upon this forethought to have a vehicle or mount waiting for you and your companions, or to have a reservation available at a venue. You pay any appropriate costs. For exceptional requests, you may need to make a Persuasion check or owe someone a favour.

optionalfeature.name: Run Silent Run Deep
entries: Once per hour, when you succeed on a Stealth check you can discreetly direct your allies. For the next minute, each ally who attempts a check to traverse the same area gains an expertise die.
When moving through an area you're familiar with or have had time to study maps of, you gain an expertise die on Stealth checks.

optionalfeature.name: Student of Technology
entries: You gain an expertise die on Magiscience checks to build, repair, or understand a technological device.
